title.rendered: AMI Delivers Trusted Firmware Solutions for NVIDIA Grace-Blackwell Platforms, Accelerating AI Infrastructure Deployment

content.rendered:
As a global leader in UEFI and BMC firmware, AMI continues to expand its comprehensive support for NVIDIA® Grace-Blackwell accelerated computing platforms, reinforcing its position as the preferred firmware partner for NVIDIA’s server and AI PC solutions.

AMI’s Aptio® and MegaRAC® firmware offerings are now fully integrated across NVIDIA’s NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs and NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra GPUs with platforms spanning data center, rack, server, and AI PC systems. These solutions are designed to meet the rigorous demands of modern data centers and AI workloads, offering unmatched reliability, scalability, and performance.

“AMI is proud to be a trusted route to market for NVIDIA’s firmware enablement,” said Sanjoy Maity, CEO of AMI. “Our collaboration ensures that partners and customers can achieve timely deployment of NVIDIA platforms with confidence, knowing they have access to production-grade firmware, long-term support, open-source / OpenBMC™ alignment, and engineering expertise.”

Accelerating Time-to-Market with Proven Solutions

Our production-quality firmware stack enables server and PC manufacturers to accelerate platform development and deployment. Leveraging AMI’s early work with NVIDIA, industry partners continue to benefit from AMI’s deep platform and firmware expertise – to drive reference design solutions across various NVIDIA Grace, Intel®, and AMD CPU-based designs.

Whether considering x86 motherboard integrations with NVIDIA GPUs or NVIDIA CPU-GPU complete systems, AMI provides a complete portfolio of boot, BMC, and security firmware solutions that meet design and performance requirements delivered at time-to-market. AMI ensures seamless integration through a comprehensive suite of firmware solutions, for an accelerated platform bring-up and development process for OEM/ODMs.

Selected designs and configurations supported by AMI include:

  • AI & HPC Server Platforms
    • NVIDIA CPU + GPUs: 4, 36, and 72 NVIDIA Blackwell GPU Configurations
    • x86 CPU + NVIDIA GPUs: NVIDIA HGX B300, NVIDIA MGX with NVIDIA ConnectX-8 SuperNIC-based PCIe Switch
  • AI Supercomputer PCs
    • Desktop: NVIDIA DGX Station, NVIDIA DGX Spark
Built for Data Center Efficiency

AMI solutions are engineered for today’s workloads, offering advanced features for power optimization, GPU and accelerator management, scalable AI infrastructure management, and sustainability. Our firmware conforms to NVIDIA performance and qualification compliance standards, ensuring compatibility, performance, and industry alignment across the ecosystem.

For more information about AMI’s firmware solutions, engineering support, documentation, or training resources on NVIDIA platforms, please visit or contact us at https://www.ami.com.

Catch Up on Related NVIDIA News and Announcements from AMI:
  • https://www.ami.com/blog/2025/06/12/ami-to-deliver-cutting-edge-uefi-and-bmc-firmware-for-nvidia-dgx-station/
  • https://www.ami.com/blog/2025/06/03/amis-aptio-v-uefi-firmware-selected-for-the-worlds-smallest-ai-supercomputer-the-nvidia-dgx-spark-personal-ai-computer/
  • https://www.ami.com/blog/2024/10/02/ami-completes-nvidia-qualified-program-software-and-system-test-suite-for-megarac-sp-x-on-nvidia-mgx-with-grace-and-grace-hopper-superchip-based-server-platforms/

NVIDIA is a registered trademark of NVIDIA Corporation. Aptio and MegaRAC are registered trademarks of AMI. OpenBMC is a trademark of LF Projects, LLC. Intel is a registered trademark of Intel Corporation. AMD is a trademark Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. All other registered trademarks and trademarks are the property of their respective companies.
